checklist_item.title: Is common_prefix_length complexity bounded by O(N*L)?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Create pathological key distributions sharing long prefixes.
2. Measure common_prefix_length computation time per recursive level.
3. Verify no quadratic or worse behavior.
4. Test at block gas limit boundaries.
5. Compare computation time to expected gas costs.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0035 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001, tb-002

checklist_item.title: Is state sync memory bounded for deeply nested branch nodes?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Craft trie nodes with maximum depth and branching.
2. Send these nodes during state sync.
3. Monitor memory consumption on receiving node.
4. Verify stack depth limits are enforced.
5. Test for memory exhaustion or stack overflow.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0036 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-004

checklist_item.title: Does encode_node explicitly handle all Fusaka/Osaka types?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. List all Fusaka-introduced types (e.g., EIP-7702 transactions).
2. Verify each has explicit isinstance check in encode_node.
3. Submit transactions of each type and trace encoding path.
4. Verify no Fusaka type reaches previous_trie.encode_node.
5. Test encoding consistency across client implementations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0037 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-005

checklist_item.title: Does trie_set use type-safe equality for default value comparison?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Identify the default value type for each trie.
2. Craft values with pathological equality behavior.
3. Attempt trie_set with these values.
4. Verify type coercion does not cause incorrect deletion.
5. Test with b'' vs None, 0 vs empty, etc.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0038 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001

checklist_item.title: Does encode_node always produce non-empty output?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Test encode_node with all valid trie value types.
2. Verify output is never b''.
3. Focus on Fusaka-introduced types.
4. Check for assertion in _prepare_trie catching empty output.
5. Attempt to create state triggering empty encoding.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0039 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001

checklist_item.title: Does trie._data only contain non-default values?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Perform various trie_set operations.
2. After each operation, verify all keys in _data map to non-default values.
3. Test sequences of set-to-nondefault followed by set-to-default.
4. Verify default values are properly deleted, not persisted.
5. Compute state root and verify consistency.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0040 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001

checklist_item.title: Is isinstance check ordering in encode_node correct for subclasses?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Identify all type hierarchies in encode_node isinstance checks.
2. Verify more specific subclasses are checked before base classes.
3. Submit transactions with Fusaka types that are subclasses.
4. Trace encoding path to verify correct branch is taken.
5. Compare across client implementations for consistent ordering.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0041 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001

checklist_item.title: Does compact encoding preserve is_leaf flag through round trips?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Encode nibble lists as leaf nodes.
2. Encode same nibble lists as extension nodes.
3. Decode each and verify is_leaf flag is preserved.
4. Craft Merkle proofs exploiting leaf/extension confusion.
5. Verify proof verification rejects ambiguous encodings.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0042 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-008

checklist_item.title: Is root_node type correct at the RLP size boundary?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Create trie states producing root_node at RLP size boundary.
2. Verify nodes with RLP >= 32 return Bytes (keccak256 hash).
3. Verify Root() constructor receives 32-byte hash in this case.
4. Test edge case at exactly 32 bytes RLP encoding.
5. Cross-check across client implementations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0043 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-003

checklist_item.title: Is get_storage_root callback deterministic for same storage state?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Call get_storage_root multiple times for same account/storage.
2. Verify identical Root value is returned each time.
3. Test with caching enabled and disabled.
4. Verify no non-determinism from internal state.
5. Cross-check across process restarts.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0046 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-006

checklist_item.title: Is shallow copy in copy_trie safe against value aliasing?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Verify all value types stored in tries are immutable.
2. Check if Fusaka introduces new mutable value types.
3. If mutable types exist, verify deep copy is used.
4. Test aliasing by modifying copied trie values.
5. Verify original trie is unaffected by modifications.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0047 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-007

checklist_item.title: Is Osaka account encoding field order correct?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Review encode_account implementation.
2. Verify field order: nonce, balance, storage_root, code_hash.
3. Submit transactions modifying accounts.
4. Compare encoded account across client implementations.
5. Verify identical encoding for identical account state.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0048 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001, tb-003

checklist_item.title: Does nibble_list_to_compact handle empty nibble lists correctly?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Encode empty nibble list with is_leaf=False.
2. Verify output is single byte 0x00.
3. Encode empty nibble list with is_leaf=True.
4. Verify output is single byte 0x20.
5. Test keys exactly matching branch level boundaries.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0049 from PROP-W1B18-1

checklist_item.title: Do all client implementations produce identical state roots for same state?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Execute identical transaction sequences on multiple clients.
2. Compare resulting state roots across all clients.
3. Test with Fusaka-specific transactions and edge cases.
4. Identify any divergence points.
5. This is the overarching consensus safety property.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0050 from PROP-W1B18-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001, tb-002, tb-003, tb-007

checklist_item.title: Does txpool reject transactions with gasLimit > 16777216 (EIP-7825)?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Submit a transaction with gasLimit = 16777217 via P2P.
2. Verify the transaction is rejected by the txpool.
3. Submit a transaction with gasLimit = 16777216 - verify acceptance.
4. Test at exact boundary: 16777215, 16777216, 16777217.
5. Verify consistent behavior across all transaction types.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0001 from PROP-W1B2-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001

checklist_item.title: Is GAS_CAP constant exactly 16777216 (2^24)?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Locate GAS_CAP constant definition in client code.
2. Verify value equals 16777216 (0x1000000, 2^24).
3. Verify constant is immutable and requires hard fork to change.
4. Compare across all client implementations.
5. Ensure no configuration overrides this value.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0002 from PROP-W1B2-1, Trust Boundary: tb-007

checklist_item.title: Is gas cap independent of block gas limit?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Set block gas limit to 30000000 (above transaction cap).
2. Submit transaction with gasLimit = 20000000.
3. Verify transaction is rejected (exceeds 16777216 cap).
4. Confirm cap is not confused with block gas limit.
5. Test on blocks with varying gas limits.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0003 from PROP-W1B2-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001, tb-003

checklist_item.title: Does block validation iterate ALL transactions for gas cap check?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Create a block with oversized tx at position 0 - verify rejection.
2. Create a block with oversized tx at last position - verify rejection.
3. Create a block with oversized tx in middle - verify rejection.
4. Verify no early-exit optimization skips transactions.
5. Test with blocks containing 1, 10, 100+ transactions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0004 from PROP-W1B2-1, Trust Boundary: tb-003

checklist_item.title: Is entire block rejected when any transaction exceeds gas cap?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Create block with 99 valid txs and 1 oversized tx.
2. Submit block via P2P.
3. Verify entire block is rejected.
4. Verify no partial acceptance occurs.
5. Confirm no valid transactions from the block are processed.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0005 from PROP-W1B2-1, Trust Boundary: tb-003

checklist_item.title: Do all transactions in valid blocks have gasLimit <= 16777216?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Extract all transactions from accepted blocks.
2. Verify each transaction.gasLimit <= 16777216.
3. This is a post-condition of block validation.
4. Test with blocks containing maximum-sized transactions.
5. Verify execution engine only processes capped transactions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0006 from PROP-W1B2-1, Trust Boundary: tb-004

checklist_item.title: Is gas cap enforced at both txpool and block validation layers?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Verify txpool admission check for gas cap.
2. Verify block validation check for gas cap.
3. Bypass txpool (private channels) and verify block validation catches.
4. Confirm both layers use identical cap value.
5. Document defense-in-depth model.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0007 from PROP-W1B2-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001, tb-002, tb-003

checklist_item.title: Is cap value 16777216 identical at txpool and block validation?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Locate gas cap constant at txpool layer.
2. Locate gas cap constant at block validation layer.
3. Verify both reference the same constant or identical value.
4. Check for any code path using different values.
5. Test boundary behavior at both layers for consistency.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0008 from PROP-W1B2-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001, tb-003

checklist_item.title: Can rejected transactions reach block builder via race condition?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Submit oversized transactions concurrently with block building.
2. Attempt to exploit TOCTOU race between rejection and building.
3. Verify rejected transactions never appear in built blocks.
4. Test under high load conditions.
5. Verify atomicity of rejection decision.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0009 from PROP-W1B2-1, Trust Boundary: tb-002

checklist_item.title: Does gas cap apply uniformly to all transaction types?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Test gas cap for type 0 (legacy) - verify enforcement.
2. Test gas cap for type 1 (EIP-2930) - verify enforcement.
3. Test gas cap for type 2 (EIP-1559) - verify enforcement.
4. Test gas cap for type 3 (EIP-4844 blob) - verify enforcement.
5. Verify no transaction type bypasses the cap.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0010 from PROP-W1B2-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001, tb-003

checklist_item.title: Does multiplication_complexity always return >= 16 (EIP-7883)?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Test with base_length=0, modulus_length=0.
2. Test with various small length combinations.
3. Verify minimum output is always 16.
4. Attempt to find inputs producing complexity < 16.
5. Test boundary values around the 32-byte threshold.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0011 from PROP-W1B2-1, Trust Boundary: tb-005
